title: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK
summary: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK  Riot Games
content:
We’re welcoming KICK to our global roster of streaming platforms, giving players around the world more ways to watch, co-stream, and engage with our games and esports. KICK is the primary platform for many passionate esports fans, especially in LATAM, MENA, and Europe. Bringing KICK into the mix of our global platform lineup helps us better serve those communities and brings more players together around the moments, matches, and creators they care about most.

Starting with MSI 2026, KICK will carry live broadcasts for global and regional events across LoL Esports, VALORANT, and TFT (excluding China and Korea).

In addition to expanding where fans can watch, this partnership also opens up more opportunities for creators. KICK streamers will have new ways to grow their communities and connect with Riot through ourexpanding creator and partner programsacross League, VALORANT, TFT, and Wild Rift.

The viewing experience on KICK will feel familiar to players, with access to the same drops and incentives available during our global event broadcasts on other platforms. As for KICK  streamers, we’ll also apply the same moderation practices and standards creators on other platforms are held to, ensuring a safe and positive experience for all.

title: These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters
summary: These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters  Red Bull
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

In Europe's LEC, the atmosphere is electric as teams prepare to integrate these updates into their drafts. G2 Esports and Fnatic have historically led the charge in innovative pocket picks and creative map rotations. This new development could provide the perfect opportunity for rising rosters to challenge the established order and secure crucial championship points for the upcoming international events.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters  Red Bull' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: T1 continue strong start at Mid-Season Invitational
summary: T1 continue strong start at Mid-Season Invitational  Field Level Media
content:
T1 completed a 3-0 sweep of Karmine Corp on Monday to advance to Round 4 of the play-in stage at the League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational in Daejeon, South Korea.

T1 completed the Round 2 wins in 31 minutes, 35 minutes and 27 minutes. Karmine Corp dropped to Round 3 to face Team Liquid, with the winner meeting T1.

The play-in stage runs through Wednesday, with four teams competing in a double-elimination bracket. Teams are seeded according to results from the First Stand Tournament and are playing best-of-five matches. The eventual winner goes to the bracket stage, an eight-team tournament that provides a $2 million prize pool including a $500,000 check for the first-place finisher.

Soo-hwan “Peyz” Kim led T1 with 30 kills against Karmine Corp, which got 13 kills each from Chang-dong “Canna” Kim and Martin “Yike” Sundelin.

Team Liquid swept into Round 3 by eliminating Deep Cross Gaming, winning in 34, 33 and 25 minutes on Monday.

Brandon Joel “Josedeodo” Villegas and Hyeon-seung “Quid” Lim paced Team Liquid with 19 kills each. Three players had 10 kills in a losing effort for DCG.

content:

title: LoL Esports |
content:
The 2026 League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational is headed to Daejeon, South Korea! MSI is the season’s midyear proving ground, where the best teams from every region collide to test their strength on the road to Worlds. With the MSI title and additional World Championship slots on the line, every match carries real consequences. Pros lock in on a rival—an enemy region, a former teammate, a lane opponent—and every game is a measure of dominance. This is MSI: rivalry-fueled, character-driven, and unapologetically bold.

The Official Rules of the 2026 Mid-Season Invitational can be foundhere.

MSI has two stages: the Play-In Stage (June 28-July 1) and the Bracket Stage (July 3-6, July 8-12). If you’re joining us in person at the event, please check out theRiot Games event policy.

This year, the teams competing from each of the different regions are:

Team Secret Whales (TSW)Revolve Deep Cross Gaming (DCG)

The format for MSI 2026 will look very similar to MSI 2025, with the only changes occurring in the Play-In Stage. This year, Play-In Stage will feature a 4-team Double-Elimination Bracket that will qualify a single team into the Bracket Stage. The Bracket Stage will remain an 8-team Double-Elimination Bracket.

title: Worlds 2026 Ticket Sales
content:
To help combat bots and prioritize players, we’re bringing back our Fan First Verification system for Worlds 2026 ticket sales. Through this process, fans can sign up for a chance to access the ticket queue.

Fans can visitlolesports.com/ticketsto sign in using their Riot ID and select their preferred ticket window based on event stage and location. Email addresses will be used to verify Riot Accounts, including validation of in-game behavior, as well as deliver ticketing access codes and important updates about the Worlds 2026 event. Your Ticketmaster account email addressMUSTmatch your Riot Games account email address in order to complete ticket purchase

Registrationopens from June 1 at 8:00AM PDT and closes June 30 at 8:00AM PDT.

If your account is verified, you will receive an email to the account associated with your Riot ID with a unique, one-time access code to Ticketmaster’s sale page. You will be placed in a “Smart Queue'' virtual line and be prompted to enter your access code to purchase tickets.

Pay close attention to when your ticket purchase window is active; you will have 24 hours to complete your purchase.Once your ticket purchase window has closed, you will no longer have access to the Fan First Access sale.

Presale ticket access will begin onJuly 22 at 8:00 AM PDTfor eligible, verified fans.
